Keep markdown formatting. Translate frontmatter values: language, title, subTitle, cover_alt, cover_credit (none). So language: English -> Spanish. Title: "The Last to Think" -> translate. SubTitle: Human or Machine? -> translate. Keep other fields unchanged. We must preserve YAML order. So modify language: Spanish, title: "El último en pensar"? Might be "El último en pensar". SubTitle: "¿Humano o máquina?" Keep quotes. Now translate body. Keep footnote asterisk. Keep italic formatting. Keep bold. Keep

. Let's translate: "Every generation discovers its technological bogeyman. Some new device emerges, and suddenly civilization teeters on the brink of idiocy. You could set your Apple Watch by these panics—assuming it has a charge. Lest we resort to analog timekeeping." Spanish: "Cada generación descubre su monstruo tecnológico. Aparece un nuevo dispositivo y, de pronto, la civilización se tambalea al borde de la idiotez. Podrías sincronizar tu Apple Watch con estos temores—suponiendo que tenga batería. Antes de que nos veamos obligados a recurrir a la medición de tiempo analógica." Continue. "Today's panic centers on AI, but before we hyperventilate about 12-year-olds who couldn't write an analog 'thank you' note yet can "write" a deep philosophical paper on the themes of justice and redemption in The Brothers Karamazov, let's check our historical receipts." Translate: "La preocupación actual gira en torno a la IA, pero antes de hiperventilar por niños de 12 años que no podían escribir una nota de agradecimiento analógica y ya pueden “escribir” un profundo ensayo filosófico sobre los temas de justicia y redención en *Los hermanos Karamazov*, revisemos nuestras pruebas históricas." Continue. "From CliffsNotes to calculators, each new technology has sparked fears of intellectual decay. In the 1940s, educators warned that CliffsNotes would make students lazy readers. In the 1950s, comic books were blamed for juvenile delinquency. The 1980s saw calculators branded as "crutches that will hobble a generation." The internet was once feared as the end of attention spans, yet it democratized knowledge and connected rural students to world-class resources. Each time, society adapted rather than collapsed." Translate. "Consider GPS—the doomsayers were technically right. Most people under 40 can't read paper maps. But instead of becoming wayward travellers, we gained the ability to navigate more often, in any city worldwide, all while avoiding traffic. Who is hand-wringing about the loss of map-reading skills now? No one. We adapted." Translate. "AI will surely follow this pattern.* Yes, it can process vast information and generate human-like text at inhuman speeds. No, it can't replicate human emotional intelligence or creativity. The challenge isn't preventing its use but **teaching its effective application**, building literacy around it. **Students need to understand** when AI amplifies learning versus short-circuits development. **Educators must redesign assessments** for an AI-augmented world." Translate. "We simply can't expect the old methods to work in this new landscape." Translate. "We survived calculators, CliffsNotes, and (so far) TikTok. We'll survive ChatGPT. The kids will adapt. I hope the adults don't take too long to catch up." Translate. Footnote: "* _Assuming we can improve the environmental impact, and avoid a dystopian nightmare. But that's a topic for another day._" Translate. Make sure to keep markdown formatting: **bold**, *italic*, etc. Now produce final output with YAML frontmatter at top. Check frontmatter: language: English -> Spanish. Title: "The Last to Think" -> "El último en pensar". subTitle: Human or Machine? -> "¿Humano o máquina?" Keep quotes? In YAML, title is quoted. Keep same quoting style. Thus: --- language: Spanish title: "El último en pensar" subTitle: "¿Humano o máquina?" category: AI subCategory: Policy date: 2025-05-31 modified: 2025-06-02 tags: [ edtech, education, technology, history ] social_image: mobile-social.webp cover_full_width: banner-thinking-decay-wide.webp cover_mobile: banner-thinking-decay-square-200.webp cover_icon: banner-thinking-decay-square-200.webp --- Then body.
